message:
feat(ma): maximum_fill_value parity for MaskedArray (franken_numpy-ydgx)

Add the NumPy-matching `maximum_fill_value` sentinel path for
MaskedArray so max-reduction over masked data uses the opposite-
polarity identity of `minimum_fill_value` and matches
np.ma.maximum_fill_value exactly.

crates/fnp-ufunc/src/lib.rs:
- MaskedArray::maximum_fill_value() forwards to the dtype helper,
  mirroring the existing minimum_fill_value() method so callers have
  a parallel API surface.
- New free function ma_maximum_fill_value(a) delegating to
  ma_maximum_fill_value_for_dtype(a.dtype()) for the dtype-only case.
- New ma_maximum_fill_value_for_dtype(dtype) returning the scalar
  identity that keeps masked entries from affecting a maximum
  reduction, polarity-flipped from minimum_fill_value:
    - Bool -> false
    - signed ints -> TYPE::MIN
    - unsigned ints -> 0
    - floats -> -inf
    - complex -> (-inf, -inf)
    - datetime/timedelta -> i64::MIN + 1 (one past NaT, matching NumPy)
- Tests exercise every dtype branch plus the MaskedArray method path
  via the existing UFuncArray scaffold so we lock the sentinel values
  against NumPy's documented behavior.

.beads/issues.jsonl:
- franken_numpy-9ber (minimum_fill_value python parity) moved to
  in_progress — the wrapper landing here validates the core behavior
  before exposure through fnp-python.
- franken_numpy-ydgx (maximum_fill_value parity) opened and marked
  in_progress for this work.
- franken_numpy-lqpc (masked_invalid wrapper parity) remains open,
  ordered after this for sequencing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
